senses.definition: A genus or species name in scientific classification; used as a Latin or scientific term for certain fish, often in taxonomy.
examples: The scientist cited the tiktaaliks when discussing the fossil record.
In the paper, the tiktaaliks was referenced as an example of transitional forms.

senses.definition: A proper noun used as a name or label in some languages or contexts; may refer to a place, person, or brand depending on usage.
examples: The dancer named Tikur performed in the festival.
In the document, Tikur is listed as a participant.

senses.definition: Used to indicate a direction, destination, or recipient, often meaning toward or to.
examples: She walked til the station.
The boat sails til dawn.

senses.definition: To tilt or cause to tilt; to slant or incline.
examples: Tilt the board to see the mark more clearly.
The photograph tilts slightly to the left.

senses.definition: A device or piece of equipment; a tool or appliance used for a specific purpose.
examples: The new tilaite improves data collection.
Retailers showcased the tilaite at the tech fair.

senses.definition: A mark or religious symbol placed on the forehead, often in Hindu tradition, signifying blessing or auspiciousness.
examples: The priest applied a tilak during the ceremony.
People wore a tilak for the festival.

senses.definition: An alternate spelling of tilak; a religious mark or adornment on the forehead in some traditions.
examples: He wore a tilaka/tilaka as part of the pilgrimage.
The tilaka signified devotion.

senses.definition: Plural form of tilak or tilaka; multiple forehead marks used in religious or ceremonial contexts.
examples: The group wore tilaks for the festival; many had bright tilaks.
Marked with tilaks, they gathered at the temple.

senses.definition: A common freshwater fish species often raised for food; mild-flavored and widely farmed.
examples: Tilapia is a popular fish for dinners.
They cook tilapia with lemon and herbs.

senses.definition: Plural of tilapia; multiple tilapia fish.
examples: The market sold several tilapias today.
They grilled two tilapias for dinner.

senses.definition: A mineral or geological term; a substance or mineral component found in tilasite formations; may refer to a specific mineral species in a niche field.
examples: The rock contained traces of tilasite.
Geologists studied tilasite in the quarry.

senses.definition: Plural form of Tilbury, a place name or a surname; may also refer to the historic Tilbury in England or other uses as a proper noun.
examples: They visited the Tilburies during the trip.
The Tilburies were listed among the attendees.

senses.definition: A town in Essex, England, historically important as a port; also a surname or place name; can refer to a type of gun emplacement in military history.
examples: Tilbury Fort guards the Thames estuary.
They stopped at Tilbury on the way to the coast.

senses.definition: A diacritical mark (~) used in some writing systems to indicate particular sounds or tones; also a name for the character in computing contexts.
examples: In programming, the tilde is represented by a tilda symbol.
The word contains a tilde over the a in tilda.

senses.definition: A diacritical mark (~) used in some languages to indicate approximate values or particular pronunciation.
examples: The tilde over n in español shows its ny sound.
In mathematics, a tilde can indicate similarity.

senses.definition: A proper noun or surname; may refer to a person named Tilden or a place named Tilden.
examples: The exhibit was curated by Dr. Tilden.
They vacationed in Tilden last summer.

senses.definition: Plural of tilde; multiple diacritical marks used in various languages.
examples: The Spanish word includes several tildes.
They studied the tildes in the language course.

senses.definition: A flat piece of material (ceramic, stone, etc.) used for covering floors, walls, or roofs.
examples: We walked on the tiled floor.
The bathroom has blue tiles.

senses.definition: To cover with tiles or to form into tiles.
examples: They tiled the kitchen walls.
The floor was tiled last year.

senses.definition: Covered with tiles.
examples: The tiled bathroom looks clean.
They installed tiled floors in the foyer.

senses.definition: A long, slender marine fish of the tilefish family, known for its firm flesh and large head.
examples: Tilefish is prized by some chefs for its delicate flavor.
They caught a tilefish off the coast.

senses.definition: Plural of tilefish.
examples: The menu featured several tilefishes from the market.
They studied tilefishes in the aquarium.

senses.definition: Having the appearance or qualities of tiles; flat and rectangular in shape.
examples: The pattern is tilelike in its arrangement.
The decorative panels are tilelike.

senses.definition: A person or company that makes tiles.
examples: The tilemaker produced durable ceramic tiles.
We hired a local tilemaker for the project.

senses.definition: The process of producing tiles.
examples: Tilemaking requires kilns and glaze technology.
They discussed tilemaking techniques at the trade show.

senses.definition: A person who installs tiles in buildings.
examples: The tiler laid the bathroom tiles.
We hired a skilled tiler for the project.

senses.definition: A workshop or shop where tiles are made or sold; a place where tilers work.
examples: The tileries across town showcased handmade tiles.
They visited a tilery to see the process.
